name: Transformation
summary: Transformations are a standard construct in the 3D world for representing the position, rotation, and sizing of a given entity. In the SketchUp world, Sketchup::ComponentInstance and Sketchup::Group have a .transformation method that reports their current state and various methods (.move!, transformation=, etc.) that allow them to be manipulated. Use of the transformation class requires a knowledge of geometrical transformations in 3 dimensions which is covered extensively on the internet.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: You can use this method or one of the more specific methods for creating specific kinds of Transformations.
constructors.remarks: You can use this method or one of the more specific methods for creating specific kinds of Transformations.

methods.name: axes
methods.signature: axes(origin, xaxis, yaxis, zaxis) => Geom::Transformation | axes(origin, xaxis, yaxis) => Geom::Transformation
methods.summary: The axes method creates a transformation that goes from world coordinates to an arbitrary coordinate system defined by an origin and three axis vectors.
methods.remarks: The axes method creates a transformation that goes from world coordinates to an arbitrary coordinate system defined by an origin and three axis vectors.

throws.when: if any of the vectors are zero length.

methods.name: interpolate
methods.summary: The interpolate method is used to create a new transformation that is the result of interpolating between two other transformations.
methods.remarks: The interpolate method is used to create a new transformation that is the result of interpolating between two other transformations. Parameter weight is a value (between 0.0 and 1.0) that represents the percentage given to `transformation1` and `transformation2`.

methods.name: rotation
methods.summary: The rotation method is used to create a transformation that does rotation about an axis.
methods.remarks: The rotation method is used to create a transformation that does rotation about an axis. The axis is defined by a point and a vector. The angle is given in radians.

methods.name: scaling
methods.signature: scaling(scale) => Geom::Transformation | scaling(xscale, yscale, zscale) => Geom::Transformation | scaling(point, scale) => Geom::Transformation | scaling(point, xscale, yscale, zscale) => Geom::Transformation
methods.summary: The scaling method is used to create a transformation that does scaling.
methods.remarks: The scaling method is used to create a transformation that does scaling.

params.name: scale
params.doc: The global scale factor for the transform.

methods.name: translation
methods.summary: The translation method is used to create a transformation that does translation.
methods.remarks: The translation method is used to create a transformation that does translation.

methods.signature: *(point) => Geom::Point3d | *(vector) => Geom::Vector3d | *(transformation) => Geom::Transformation | *(point) => Array(Float, Float, Float) | *(plane) => Array(Float, Float, Float, Float) | *(plane) => Array(Float, Float, Float, Float)
methods.summary: The #* method is used to do matrix multiplication using the transform.
methods.remarks: The #* method is used to do matrix multiplication using the transform.

methods.name: clone
methods.summary: The #clone method is used to create a copy of a transformation.
methods.remarks: The #clone method is used to create a copy of a transformation.

methods.name: identity?
methods.summary: Note: As of SketchUp 2018, this now looks at the data to determine if the transformation is identity.
methods.remarks: Note: As of SketchUp 2018, this now looks at the data to determine if the transformation is identity. Prior to SU2018, this only looks at the flag to see if the transform has not been modified. If the transform has been changed, this will return false even if it is really the identity. The #identity? method is used to determine if a transformation is the IDENTITY transform.

returns.doc: true if the transformation is the identity

methods.name: inverse
methods.summary: Note: As of SketchUp 2026, this will raise an error if the Geom::Transformation is not invertible.
methods.remarks: Note: As of SketchUp 2026, this will raise an error if the Geom::Transformation is not invertible. Prior to 2026 this would silently attempt to invert the transformation possibly returning in an invalid transformation. The #inverse method is used to retrieve the inverse of a transformation.

methods.name: invert!
methods.summary: Note: As of SketchUp 2026, this will raise an error if the Geom::Transformation is not invertible.
methods.remarks: Note: As of SketchUp 2026, this will raise an error if the Geom::Transformation is not invertible. Prior to 2026 this would silently attempt to invert the transformation possibly creating in an invalid transformation. The #invert! method sets the transformation to its inverse.

methods.name: origin
methods.summary: The #origin method retrieves the origin of a rigid transformation.
methods.remarks: The #origin method retrieves the origin of a rigid transformation.

methods.name: set!
methods.signature: set!(transformation) => Geom::Transformation | set!(point) => Geom::Transformation | set!(vector) => Geom::Transformation | set!(matrix) => Geom::Transformation | set!(scale) => Geom::Transformation
methods.summary: The #set! method is used to set this transformation to match another one.
methods.remarks: The #set! method is used to set this transformation to match another one. The argument is anything that can be converted into a transformation.

methods.name: to_a
methods.summary: The #to_a method retrieves a 16 element array which contains the values that define the transformation.
methods.remarks: The #to_a method retrieves a 16 element array which contains the values that define the transformation.

methods.name: xaxis
methods.summary: The #xaxis method retrieves the x axis of a rigid transformation.
methods.remarks: The #xaxis method retrieves the x axis of a rigid transformation.

methods.name: yaxis
methods.summary: The #yaxis method retrieves the y axis of a rigid transformation.
methods.remarks: The #yaxis method retrieves the y axis of a rigid transformation.

methods.name: zaxis
methods.summary: The #zaxis method retrieves the z axis of a rigid transformation.
methods.remarks: The #zaxis method retrieves the z axis of a rigid transformation.
